title: He Ran Down 100 Stairs on His Hands. 44 Seconds. New Record.
script: Down 100 stairs. On hands? Hari Chandra Giri from Nepal just pulled off what Guinness called his 'most dangerous stunt yet.' On June 9th, he descended 100 stairs — entirely on his hands — in 44.71 seconds. New world record. And that sounds terrifying until you realize this is all he does. He already holds Guinness records for the fastest 50-stair handwalk, the fastest 75-stair handwalk, and variations involving footballs and distances. He started in 2021. He gets faster every time. At Yunyang, China, he balanced on his hands at the top of a 100-step staircase and just — went. 44 seconds later, record broken. Giri said one wrong placement and it's head-first all the way down. So when does Hari Chandra Giri run out of stairs to conquer?
